title: lupinus arizonicus
intro: Lupinus arizonicus, also known as Arizona lupine, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a member of the legume family and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds. The health benefits of Lupinus arizonicus include its potential to help lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and alleviate symptoms of menopause. Lupinus arizonicus can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and its herbal preparations are often used to promote cardiovascular health and support the immune system.
